text.title: Prompt Version Pinning Runbook — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-version-pinning-runbook methodology. Sources: Portkey LLM gateway documentation; Helicone open-source proxy; LangChain Hub prompt versioning.

rule.statement: Prompts are content-addressable objects: `{prompt_id, version}` is immutable; never edit a published version in-place — bump the version.
rule.rationale: Mutable prompt versions defeat replay, audit, and rollback simultaneously; immutability is the load-bearing primitive.

rule.statement: The LLM gateway rejects any LLM call that does not declare `{prompt_id, version}`; enforcement at the gateway is non-negotiable.
rule.rationale: Client-side enforcement leaks the moment one team forgets to ship an update; only the gateway sees 100% of traffic.

rule.statement: Declare the rollout strategy per change: shadow (no traffic, eval only), canary (5%), guarded-flag (per-tenant), or full; default is never `full`.
rule.rationale: Defaulting to full rollout is how silent regressions ship; explicit strategy forces a deliberate choice.

rule.statement: `gateway rollback <prompt_id>` returns the prompt to the last known-good version in < 60 seconds, with traffic shift complete within 1 minute.
rule.rationale: MTTR for prompt regressions is the customer-visible signal; under-60s rollback is the SLO that distinguishes pro from amateur.

rule.statement: Hold a weekly A/B review covering every prompt under active comparison; promote, demote, or kill each comparison explicitly.
rule.rationale: Without scheduled review, A/Bs accumulate as silent state and prompt drift goes unnoticed.
